headline: A Miracle in the Rubble: New Life Discovered After Venezuelan Earthquakes
meta_description: After surviving a devastating 7.5 magnitude earthquake, a pregnant woman receives prenatal care at a shelter in Caracas, offering hope amid tragedy.
summary_short: Dayra Morales survived being trapped under rubble for two hours. Days later, she discovered she was pregnant—and her unborn baby was unharmed.
body:
When two catastrophic earthquakes struck central-northern Venezuela on 24 June, Roberto Rodríguez watched his building collapse floor by floor. His partner, Dayra Morales, was pinned under the rubble from her feet downward. It took neighbours two hours to free her.

The disaster claimed almost 5,000 lives and left thousands homeless, yet something extraordinary emerged from the devastation. Days after her rescue, Ms. Morales learned she was pregnant—and remarkably, the baby had survived intact, despite her falling from the sixth floor of a 12-story building.

"I consider it a true miracle," Mr. Rodríguez said.

Now sheltering at the José María Vargas sports complex in Caracas alongside over 1,000 other displaced people, Ms. Morales receives prenatal care from a UNFPA-supported clinic. The UN agency has mobilised reproductive health services across three shelters, providing consultations and essential supplies to survivors.

Among the volunteers is Yoseys Escalona, a tent representative coordinating mutual aid among the 12 families sharing her shelter. Despite losing homes and loved ones, these women continue supporting one another—a resilience that transforms tragedy into collective strength, one act of kindness at a time.
